ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "version_control_system" TEXT /* Version control system used for the product */,
  "programming_languages" TEXT /* Programming languages used for the product */,
  "technology_stack" TEXT /* Technology stack used for the product */,
  "product_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product manager responsible for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product this version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_versions_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lifecycle_phases" (
  "phase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lifecycle phase */,
  "phase_name" TEXT /* Name of the lifecycle phase */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the lifecycle phase */,
  "phase_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the lifecycle phase */,
  "phase_order" INTEGER /* Order of the lifecycle phase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("phase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_lifecycle" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "phase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the lifecycle phase */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the lifecycle phase */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the lifecycle phase */,
  "lifecycle_status" TEXT /* Current status of the product lifecycle */,
  "lifecycle_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the product lifecycle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id", "phase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documentation" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product this document belongs to */,
  "doc_name" TEXT /* Name of the document */,
  "doc_type" TEXT /* Type of the document */,
  "doc_version" TEXT /* Version of the document */,
  "doc_format" TEXT /* Format of the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documentation_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product this comment belongs to */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "comment_author" TEXT /* Author of the comment */,
  "comment_reply_to" INTEGER /* ID of the comment being replied to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workflows" (
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workflow */,
  "workflow_name" TEXT /* Name of the workflow */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workflow */,
  "workflow_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the workflow */,
  "workflow_owner" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for the workflow */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workflow_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workflow_steps" (
  "step_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each step */,
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* ID of the workflow this step belongs to */,
  "step_name" TEXT /* Name of the step */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the step */,
  "step_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the step */,
  "step_status" TEXT /* Status of the step */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("step_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_workflow_steps_workflow_id FOREIGN KEY ("workflow_id") REFERENCES workflows ("workflow_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workflow_instances" (
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instance */,
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* ID of the workflow this instance belongs to */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the instance */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the instance */,
  "instance_status" TEXT /* Status of the instance */,
  "instance_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the instance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_workflow_instances_workflow_id FOREIGN KEY ("workflow_id") REFERENCES workflows ("workflow_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this notification belongs to */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of the notification */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_lifecycle_history" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "phase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the lifecycle phase */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the lifecycle phase */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the lifecycle phase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id", "phase_id")
)
